name: Gay
description: A person who is primarily attracted to people of the same gender. Commonly used to refer to gay men specifically, but also used as a broader umbrella for LGBTQ+ people.

name: Lesbian
description: A woman or woman-aligned person who is primarily attracted to other women or woman-aligned people. The term comes from the island of Lesbos, home of the ancient Greek poet Sappho.

name: Bisexual
description: A person who experiences attraction to people of their own gender and other genders. The 'bi' prefix originally referred to two genders but is now broadly understood to include attraction to multiple or all genders.

name: Transgender
description: An umbrella term for people whose gender identity differs from the sex they were assigned at birth. Includes trans men, trans women, and nonbinary people who identify with the trans experience.

name: Queer
description: A reclaimed umbrella term encompassing non-normative sexual orientations and/or gender identities. Some people use it as a specific identity; others as a general term for the community. Some older LGBTQ+ people still find the term offensive due to its historical use as a slur.

name: Intersex
description: A person born with reproductive anatomy, chromosomes, or hormones that don't fit typical definitions of male or female. Intersex is a natural biological variation, not a gender identity or sexual orientation.

name: Asexual
description: A person who experiences little or no sexual attraction to others. Asexuality is a spectrum and does not preclude romantic attraction, emotional connection, or the desire for relationships.

name: Aromantic
description: A person who experiences little or no romantic attraction to others. Aromantic people may still experience sexual attraction. Aromanticism is a spectrum and includes a range of related identities.

name: Nonbinary
description: An umbrella term for gender identities that are not exclusively 'man' or 'woman'. Nonbinary people may identify as both, neither, somewhere in between, or completely outside the gender binary.

name: Pansexual
description: A person who experiences attraction regardless of gender, or to people of all genders. Sometimes described as 'gender-blind' attraction. Many people use bisexual and pansexual interchangeably.

name: Demisexual
description: A person who experiences sexual attraction only after forming a strong emotional bond with someone. Considered part of the asexual spectrum, as sexual attraction is absent until a specific condition is met.

name: Genderfluid
description: A person whose gender identity shifts or fluctuates over time. A genderfluid person may feel like a woman on some days, a man on others, or somewhere outside the binary, with shifts occurring over varying timescales.

name: Agender
description: A person who identifies as having no gender, or who feels that the concept of gender does not apply to them. Sometimes described as 'gender-null' or 'genderless'. Some agender people also identify as transgender or nonbinary.

name: Genderqueer
description: An umbrella term for gender identities that are outside the gender binary and/or challenge traditional gender norms. Predates 'nonbinary' and is still used as a specific identity by many, particularly older community members.

name: Two-Spirit
description: A term used by some Indigenous North American peoples to describe a person who fulfills a traditional third-gender or gender-variant role. Two-Spirit is a pan-Indian term adopted in 1990 and should only be used as a self-identifier by Indigenous people.

name: Sapphic
description: An umbrella term for women and non-men who experience attraction to women and/or non-men. Includes lesbians, bisexual women, pansexual women, and others. Rooted in the legacy of the ancient Greek poet Sappho.

name: Achillean
description: An umbrella term for men and non-women who experience attraction to men and/or non-women. Includes gay men, bisexual men, pansexual men, and others. Named after the mythological hero Achilles.

name: Omnisexual
description: A person who experiences attraction to people of all genders, but in which gender does play a role in how the attraction is experienced (unlike pansexual, where gender is often described as irrelevant).

name: Polysexual
description: A person who experiences attraction to multiple genders but not necessarily all genders. Distinct from polyamorous (which refers to relationship structure). The 'poly' prefix means 'many'.

name: Graysexual
description: A person who experiences sexual attraction rarely, only under specific circumstances, or with low intensity. Exists on the spectrum between asexual and allosexual (experiencing typical levels of sexual attraction).

name: Demiromantic
description: A person who experiences romantic attraction only after forming a close emotional bond. The romantic equivalent of demisexual.

name: Polyamorous
description: A relationship structure (not a sexual orientation) in which a person has or desires multiple romantic and/or sexual partners simultaneously, with the knowledge and consent of everyone involved.

name: Relationship Anarchist
description: A relationship philosophy and practice that rejects conventional hierarchies (romantic vs. platonic, primary vs. secondary) and allows each relationship to evolve according to its own needs and values, free from societal scripts.

name: Questioning
description: A term for people who are uncertain about, exploring, or in the process of defining their sexual orientation, gender identity, or both. Often included in the LGBTQ+ acronym (LGBTQIA+).

name: Bigender
description: A person who identifies as two genders simultaneously or alternating. The two genders experienced may include any combination (e.g., man and woman, or woman and nonbinary).

name: Neutrois
description: A neutral or null gender identity, sometimes described as the desire to have no gender or to transition to a neutral physical presentation. Closely related to agender but with a distinct focus on neutral presentation.

name: Muxe
description: A third gender identity recognized in Zapotec communities of Oaxaca, Mexico. Muxes are people assigned male at birth who have a feminine gender expression and/or identity. The term has existed in Zapotec culture for centuries.

name: Hijra
description: A third gender recognized in South Asian cultures (India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Nepal). Hijras have a documented history stretching back thousands of years and in 2014 were legally recognized as a third gender in India and Pakistan.

name: Enby
description: An informal term derived from the initials 'N.B.' (nonbinary). Used as a casual, affectionate identity label within the nonbinary community. The term emerged from online LGBTQ+ communities circa 2013.

name: Androsexual
description: A person who experiences attraction to masculinity or masculine-presenting people, regardless of whether those people are cisgender men, trans men, nonbinary, or otherwise. Useful for describing attraction independent of one's own gender.

name: Gynosexual
description: A person who experiences attraction to femininity or feminine-presenting people, regardless of whether those people are cisgender women, trans women, nonbinary, or otherwise. Useful for describing attraction independent of one's own gender.
